product_overview: DigitoPub.com is a comprehensive digital platform designed for managing academic and scientific journals. It provides an end-to-end solution for journal management, manuscript submissions, peer reviews, and analytics, integrated with the Open Journal Systems (OJS) database for seamless data synchronization. The platform leverages a robust MySQL backend, modern React and Next.js frontend technologies, and features a secure JWT-based authentication system for admin access.
core_goals: Migrate the existing platform database from PostgreSQL to MySQL while maintaining data integrity and application functionality.
Integrate read-only access and periodic synchronization with an external Open Journal Systems (OJS) MySQL database for enriched journal data and analytics.
Provide a secure, role-based admin dashboard for managing journals, submissions, reviews, authors, FAQs, messages, and settings.
Implement a custom JWT-based authentication system with secure session management and middleware protection.
Create an intuitive, responsive UI using React, Next.js App Router, Tailwind CSS, and reusable Radix UI components.
Offer an automated testing widget and theming options (dark/light modes) as part of the client interface.
key_features: Complete admin dashboard with sidebar navigation, header, and protected routes for managing journals, submissions, peer reviews, authors, FAQs, messages, analytics, and settings.
Custom JWT authentication system utilizing httpOnly cookies, bcrypt password hashing, and JWT session management with secure middleware protections.
Database migration tools and guides to transfer from PostgreSQL to MySQL including data transformations (UUID to BIGINT, array to JSON, JSONB to JSON).
Integration with OJS via a read-only MySQL user with automated synchronization scripts and cron job scheduling for data consistency.
Comprehensive API routes for authentication (login, logout, register, session info) supporting secure user management.
Reusable UI components built on Radix UI and shadcn/ui for consistent and accessible interfaces.
Dark/light theme toggle implemented via next-themes for user personalization.
Client-side Testsprite widget for automated interaction testing and form simulation.
Prisma ORM integration for database schema management and query abstraction with optimized indexing and triggers.
user_flow_summary: Admin users visit the platform and authenticate via the login page using email and password, establishing a secure session with JWT stored in httpOnly cookies.

Upon successful authentication, admins access the protected admin dashboard to manage journals, submissions, reviews, authors, messages, FAQs, analytics, and settings through intuitive UI components and navigation.
Admins can view and filter lists of journals, submissions, reviews, and authors, create new entries or edit existing ones with form interfaces that communicate via API routes.
The platform periodically synchronizes journal and article data from the external OJS database through automated scripts, ensuring up-to-date content in the admin views and analytics dashboards.
Admins can toggle between dark and light mode themes globally within the application interface for usability preferences.
Automated testing is conducted using the embedded Testsprite widget to simulate user interactions and validate form workflows and UI responsiveness during development and QA.
In case of session expiry or logout, the admin is redirected to the login page, ensuring secure access control enforced by server middleware.
validation_criteria: All PostgreSQL data is correctly migrated to MySQL preserving data integrity, including correct type mappings and indexing.
The custom JWT authentication system correctly protects admin routes with appropriate session expiry, secured cookies, and password validation via bcrypt.
Admin dashboard features are fully accessible only to authenticated admin users with no unauthorized access.

Synchronization scripts between DigitoPub’s MySQL database and the OJS system successfully import journals, submissions, and article metadata without errors, both manually and via cron job automation.
UI responsiveness and theme toggling function correctly across supported browsers and device sizes.
API routes for authentication handle all edge cases such as invalid credentials, session expiration, and logout correctly with proper status codes and feedback.
Automated tests via Testsprite widget reproduce expected user form input behaviors and interactions without failures.
Database connection verification scripts for both main application and OJS databases run successfully with expected statistics outputs.
Security best practices are enforced including use of read-only users for OJS, strong password policies, HTTP-only cookies, and environment variable management.
